body.processed:
Global Relief Partners deployed emergency response teams within 12 hours of Hurricane Maria making landfall along the Gulf Coast as a Category 4 storm, bringing 145 mph winds and devastating storm surge.

Response Activities

  • Established 15 emergency shelters housing 8,400 displaced residents
  • Distributed 120,000 meals and 50,000 gallons of clean water in the first week
  • Deployed mobile medical units providing care to 3,200 patients
  • Distributed 5,000 emergency supply kits (tarps, hygiene items, flashlights)
  • Coordinated search and rescue operations with local emergency services

Ongoing Recovery

As the emergency phase transitions to recovery, our teams are focusing on temporary housing solutions, debris removal support, and connecting affected families with federal disaster assistance programs.
body.summary: Global Relief Partners deployed emergency response teams within 12 hours of Hurricane Maria making landfall along the Gulf Coast as a Category 4 storm, bringing 145 mph winds and devastating storm sur

body.processed:
A 7.1 magnitude earthquake struck central Turkey, causing widespread destruction in several provinces. Global Relief Partners activated its international rapid response team within hours.

Response Activities

  • Deployed search and rescue teams with specialized equipment
  • Established field hospitals treating 1,800 patients
  • Distributed 10,000 winterized shelter kits to families
  • Provided psychosocial support to 5,000 affected individuals
  • Set up water purification systems serving 25,000 people daily

Partnerships

We are coordinating closely with Turkish emergency management authorities (AFAD), the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA), and local NGO partners.

body.processed:
Record spring rainfall and rapid snowmelt caused catastrophic flooding across the upper Midwest, affecting communities along the Mississippi and Missouri river systems.

Response Activities

  • Distributed 25,000 sandbags and flood barriers to at-risk communities
  • Evacuated 1,200 residents from flood zones using boats and vehicles
  • Opened 8 emergency shelters with capacity for 3,000 people
  • Provided hot meals and clean water to 15,000 displaced residents
  • Deployed damage assessment teams to 12 counties

Community Support

Our case managers are working directly with flood-affected families to help them navigate the recovery process, access FEMA assistance, and develop plans for rebuilding.
body.summary: Record spring rainfall and rapid snowmelt caused catastrophic flooding across the upper Midwest, affecting communities along the Mississippi and Missouri river systems.Response ActivitiesDistributed 2
